segments.text: Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars:
She hath killed her beasts; she hath mingled her wine; she hath also furnished her table.
She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city,
Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither:
as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Come, eat of my bread, and drink of the wine which I have mingled.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked man getteth himself a blot.
Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: A foolish woman is clamorous: she is simple, and knoweth nothing.
For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,
To call passengers who go right on their ways:
Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither:
and as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,
segments.speaker: Foolish Woman

segments.text: Stolen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.
segments.speaker: Foolish Woman

segments.text: But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell.
